model_input:
You are a DVOC (Draft-Verify-Action Loop) verifier. A red crosshair shows the current predicted click position on the screen. A fixed blue circle provides spatial reference.

Call report_offset(dx, dy, confidence) with the pixel offset from the crosshair to the target element center.
dx: pixels right (negative = left). dy: pixels down (negative = up).
confidence: 0.0 (uncertain) to 1.0 (certain).

Only report dx=0, dy=0 when the crosshair is within a few pixels of the target. If uncertain, set lower confidence so the loop keeps refining.

Task: Click the Sign In button
Previous iterations:
  iter 0: at (638,124) said dx=0 dy=480 conf=1.00 -> (638,604)
  iter 1: at (638,604) said dx=2 dy=-100 conf=0.80 -> (640,504)
  iter 2: at (639,554) said dx=0 dy=-80 conf=0.90 -> (639,474)
  iter 3: at (639,534) said dx=0 dy=-80 conf=0.90 -> (639,454)
  iter 4: at (639,514) said dx=0 dy=-60 conf=0.90 -> (639,454)
Call report_offset().

raw_message.reasoning_content:
The user wants to click the "Sign In" button.
The current crosshair (red cross) is slightly below the "Sign In" button.
The "Sign In" button is the blue rectangular area.
The crosshair is currently at approximately (639, 454) based on the history, but the image shows it's just below the button.
Let's look at the image.
The button center is around (639, 450).
The crosshair is at (639, 499) approximately (looking at the bottom right text: (639, 499) +/- 30px).
Actually, the crosshair is right on the bottom edge of the button or just below it.
The text "Sign In" is above the crosshair.
The button center is slightly above the crosshair.
Distance from crosshair center to button center:
The button spans from roughly y=420 to y=480. Wait, no.
Let's re-evaluate coordinates.
Image size is 1280x720.
Button is centrally located.
Button top is around 420, bottom around 480.
Crosshair is around 499? No, the image shows it's just below the text "Sign In".
Actually, the crosshair is at (639, 499).
The "Sign In" button center is roughly at (639, 450).
Offset dy = 450 - 499 = -49.
Let's refine.
The button is from y=421 to y=479 (approx). Center y = 450.
Crosshair is at y=499.
Offset dy = -49.
Wait, let's look closer at the image.
The crosshair is almost at the bottom edge of the button.
Actually, looking at the crop, the crosshair is below the button.
The button center is higher.
Center of "Sign In" button: x=640, y=450.
Current crosshair: x=639, y=499.
dx = 1, dy = -49.
Confidence is high.
